posts.title: Why Your Edmonton Business Needs a Website That Actually Works
posts.author: Good Bones Team
posts.excerpt: A pretty homepage isn't enough. Here's what makes a website a real asset for local Edmonton businesses.
posts.body:
Your website is often the first impression potential customers get of your business. For Edmonton businesses competing in a local market, that impression has to do more than look nice—it has to work.

What does "working" mean?

A website that works is fast, accessible, and clearly guides visitors toward action. It loads in under three seconds, reads well on phones, and makes it obvious how to book, buy, or get in touch.

  • Speed: Slow sites lose visitors before they read a word.
  • Clarity: Confusing navigation sends people back to Google.
  • Trust: Outdated design makes buyers hesitate.

Built for Edmonton, ready everywhere

Good Bones builds hand-coded, strategy-first websites for Edmonton businesses that want to compete beyond the local market. No bloated templates. No mystery plugins. Just a solid foundation and a design that converts.

Start a project and let's give your business a website with good bones.

posts.title: 5 Signs Your Website Needs a Rescue
posts.author: Good Bones Team
posts.excerpt: Slow load times, broken forms, and mobile headaches are costing you customers. Here are five warning signs—and what to do about them.
posts.body:
Most small business owners know when something feels off about their website, but it is easy to ignore the symptoms until they start hurting sales. Here are five common signs your site needs a rescue.

1. It takes more than three seconds to load

Visitors expect speed. If your pages drag, people leave before they see your offer. A rescue usually starts with image optimization, code cleanup, and better hosting.

2. It looks broken on phones

More than half of local web traffic comes from mobile devices. If text is tiny, buttons are hard to tap, or layouts shift around, you are losing trust—and rankings.

3. Forms or links do not work

Broken contact forms, dead links, and checkout errors silently kill conversions. These should be found and fixed before they cost you another lead.

4. You cannot update it yourself

If changing a phone number means emailing a developer, your site is too fragile. A good rescue sets you up with a workflow you can actually manage.

5. It does not show up in local search

If customers in Edmonton cannot find you on Google, your site is not doing its job. Technical SEO, local schema, and clear content all help.

Diagnose first, fix fast

Good Bones specializes in site rescues for Edmonton businesses. We find what is broken, fix what matters most, and give you a plan to keep things running smoothly.

Book a free rescue review and stop letting your website work against you.
